title: Hermes Agentic OS is Insane... just watch
summary: Jack Roberts demonstrates how to build an agentic operating system that integrates Hermes, Claude, ChatGPT, and other AI tools in one place. The system includes features like context isolation, a dreaming function for overnight insights, persona management, memory stack, and a dashboard for monitoring AI spending and artifacts across all connected tools.
writeup: This video demonstrates how to build a visual intelligence dashboard on top of the Hermes AI agent and Claude Code, framed as an "agentic operating system" that collapses context isolation across Claude, ChatGPT, Gemini, Cursor, and other fragmented tools into one interface. The core argument is that a unified context layer unlocks capabilities unavailable from any single tool alone: overnight synthesis across all conversation histories, live cross-platform spend tracking, visual skill and persona management, and prompt-driven goal planning. The build walkthrough covers four main surfaces: mission control for multi-week goal tracking, the Pantheon for persona and model delegation, the data lake bridge between Claude Code and Hermes, and a dreaming module that runs nightly to surface usage patterns and cost optimization signals without user intervention.
key_points: The central problem the OS solves is context isolation: conversations, skills, and data are siloed across Claude, ChatGPT, Grok, Gemini, Cursor, and Antigravity, so insights that require cross-tool synthesis are simply unavailable from any one interface.

The dreaming function is an agent that runs overnight with full read access to all conversation histories across every connected platform and generates a short morning brief each day, surfacing usage patterns, underutilized skills, and goal-relevant suggestions without requiring a prompt.

Mission control is a prompt-driven goal-tracking feature: a single seed prompt sent to Hermes triggers a series of clarifying questions and produces a structured mid-term plan that distinguishes what Hermes handles autonomously from what the user must do.

The Pantheon panel provides a visual map of all personas and skills, with each persona bound to a specific model (for example, cron-job personas routed to a free model and higher-stakes work to a stronger one), allowing deliberate model delegation rather than defaulting everything to the same endpoint.

The OS tracks live AI spend by the hour and by the day across all connected platforms, and the overnight dreaming output has returned concrete cost findings such as flagging that only 20 percent of a paid plan's capacity was being used.

Connecting Hermes to Obsidian, local Claude, and Pinecone in a single memory layer means the agent carries the same rich context into every session, and the quality of that context directly determines the quality of its decisions and recommendations.
